description: A plain candid documentary photograph with no on-image text or agency graphics. The scene is inside an industrial fabrication shop. In the foreground a bearded male welder/worker (seen from behind, wearing a tan welding jacket, a do-rag, and clear safety glasses) faces a woman at right who has long dark hair and wears a blue mesh high-visibility vest with yellow reflective stripes and safety glasses; she appears to be an official or executive touring the facility (consistent with USDOL's posts featuring Labor Secretary site visits). Other workers in vests and work clothes move among large metal coils/equipment in the blurred background. Naturalistic photographic lighting and color, a real photo, not a designed meme or poster. It supports USDOL's 'American Workers First' messaging by depicting an official engaging with skilled American manufacturing workers.
